text.div:
Generated Narrative: StructureDefinition EHDSSpecimenLaboratoryObligations

Language: en

NameFlagsCard.TypeDescription & Constraints
EHDSSpecimen 0..*EHDSSpecimenSpecimen model
identifier O1..*IdentifierAn identifier of the specimen which is unique within in a defined scope. Example: identifier assigned by ordering system, identifier assigned by laboratory etc. Multiple identifiers can be used.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
status O0..1CodeableConceptAvailability of the specimen.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
type O0..1CodeableConceptThe kind of material that forms the Specimen.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
specimenSource[x] O0..1EHDSPatient, EHDSLocation, EHDSDeviceWhere the specimen came from. This may be from patient(s), from a location (e.g., the source of an environmental sample), or a device. Additional types of specimen may be allowed in implementations.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHOULD:displayConsumer
parentSpecimen O0..*EHDSSpecimenSpecimen from which this specimen originated
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
request O0..*EHDSServiceRequestWhy the specimen ws collected
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
combined O0..1CodeableConceptThis element signifies if the specimen is part of a group or pooled.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHOULD:displayConsumer
collection O0..*BaseCollection details
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
collected[x] O1..1Period, dateTimeThe period or date and time of specimen collection.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
bodySite O0..1EHDSBodyStructureAnatomic location (e.g. body location, laterality and other details) where the material is collected, e.g. Elbow, left
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
container
containerDevice O1..1EHDSDeviceThe device resource for the the container holding the specimen.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
condition O0..*CodeableConceptState of the specimen. State of the speeimen should be alligned with dataAbsentReason element of the Observatiom model.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
note O0..1stringTo communicate any details or issues about the specimen or during the specimen collection. (for example: broken vial, sent with patient, frozen).
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer

Documentation for this format

name: EHDSSpecimenLaboratoryObligations
title: Specimen Obligations
description: Obligations for the logical model of specimen

element.short: Specimen model
element.definition: Model for a specimen to be used for analysis

element.short: An identifier of the specimen which is unique within in a defined scope. Example: identifier assigned by ordering system, identifier assigned by laboratory etc. Multiple identifiers can be used.
element.definition: An identifier of the specimen which is unique within in a defined scope. Example: identifier assigned by ordering system, identifier assigned by laboratory etc. Multiple identifiers can be used.

element.short: Availability of the specimen.
element.definition: Availability of the specimen.

element.short: The kind of material that forms the Specimen.
element.definition: The kind of material that forms the Specimen.

element.short: Where the specimen came from. This may be from patient(s), from a location (e.g., the source of an environmental sample), or a device. Additional types of specimen may be allowed in implementations.
element.definition: Where the specimen came from. This may be from patient(s), from a location (e.g., the source of an environmental sample), or a device. Additional types of specimen may be allowed in implementations.

element.short: Specimen from which this specimen originated
element.definition: Specimen from which this specimen originated

element.short: Why the specimen ws collected
element.definition: Why the specimen ws collected

element.short: This element signifies if the specimen is part of a group or pooled.
element.definition: This element signifies if the specimen is part of a group or pooled.

element.short: Collection details
element.definition: Collection details

element.short: Person or organisation who collected the specimen
element.definition: Person or organisation who collected the specimen

element.short: The period or date and time of specimen collection.
element.definition: The period or date and time of specimen collection.

element.short: The quantity of specimen collected
element.definition: The quantity of specimen collected

element.short: If relevant for the results, the method of obtaining the specimen.
element.definition: If relevant for the results, the method of obtaining the specimen.

element.short: Device used to perform specimen collection
element.definition: Device used to perform specimen collection

element.short: Anatomic location (e.g. body location, laterality and other details) where the material is collected, e.g. Elbow, left
element.definition: Anatomic location (e.g. body location, laterality and other details) where the material is collected, e.g. Elbow, left

element.short: Date and time when specimen is received by the testing laboratory.
element.definition: Date and time when specimen is received by the testing laboratory.

element.short: The container holding the specimen.
element.definition: The container holding the specimen.

element.short: Quantity of specimen within container
element.definition: Quantity of specimen within container

element.short: The device resource for the the container holding the specimen.
element.definition: The device resource for the the container holding the specimen.

element.short: State of the specimen. State of the speeimen should be alligned with dataAbsentReason element of the Observatiom model.
element.definition: State of the specimen. State of the speeimen should be alligned with dataAbsentReason element of the Observatiom model.

element.short: To communicate any details or issues about the specimen or during the specimen collection. (for example: broken vial, sent with patient, frozen).
element.definition: To communicate any details or issues about the specimen or during the specimen collection. (for example: broken vial, sent with patient, frozen).
